NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF JITKA PETRICKOVA RE: NON- OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STAY CIVIL PROCEEDINGS (TRANSACTION ID # 100124484) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PETRICKOVA, JITKA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT'S AUDREY KOH AND ELIZABETH MOY, M.D.'S PETITION TO ASSIGN A NEUTRAL ARBITRATOR IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 24, 2022. THE ARBITRATION SELECTION PROCESS OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT HAS APPARENTLY BROKEN DOWN. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 1281.6. THE PARTIES SHALL IMMEDIATELY MEET AND CONFER AND SUBMIT A LIST OF 8 PROSPECTIVE ARBITRATORS TO THE COURT BY OCTOBER 14, 2022. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON THE 8 PROSPECTIVE ARBITRATORS, THEY MAY EACH SUGGEST 4. AT THE OCTOBER 24, 2022 HEARING, THE COURT WILL NOMINATE 5 ARBITRATORS IF SUCH ACTION IS STILL NECESSARY. THE COURT ENCOURAGES THE PARTIES TO RESOLVE THIS MATTER ON THEIR OWN. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

MOTION / Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Assign A Neutral Arbitrator
Off Calendar Oct-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS AUDREY KOH and ELIZABETH MOY, M.D.'S Petition To Assign A Neutral Arbitrator.

The motion to compel arbitraton and stay this case is granted. The parties have agreed to appoint Honorable Paul L. Beeman, (Ret.) to arbitrate their dispute.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION / Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Assign A Neutral Arbitrator
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Continued To Oct-24-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S AUDREY KOH and ELIZABETH MOY, M.D.'S Petition To Assign A Neutral Arbitrator.

Defendant's petition to appoint neutral arbitrator is continued to October 24, 2022. The arbitration selection process of the parties' agreement has apparently broken down. The parties are ordered to comply with CCP 1281.6. The parties shall immediately meet and confer and submit a list of 8 prospective arbitrators to the court by October 14, 2022. If the parties cannot agree on the 8 prospective arbitrators, they may each suggest 4. At the October 24, 2022 hearing, the court will nominate 5 arbitrators if such action is still necessary. The court encourages the parties to resolve this matter on their own.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
